requirements.title: Array Initialization
requirements.description: The array module shall initialize from a caller-provided API vtable and capacity.
requirements.rationale: The array is the foundational data structure upon which queue, stack, heap, and map are built; it must initialize from caller-provided storage to satisfy the zero-allocation constraint.

requirements.title: Array SetAt
requirements.description: The array shall copy a value into a slot at a given index in O(1) time.
requirements.rationale: SetAt is the fundamental write operation, using the pointer API to copy values into slots in a type-safe manner.

requirements.title: Array GetAt
requirements.description: The array shall return a pointer descriptor to the slot at a given index in O(1) time.
requirements.rationale: GetAt returns a pointer descriptor rather than a raw pointer, maintaining the type-safe access model throughout the data structure hierarchy.

requirements.title: Array RemoveAt
requirements.description: The array shall clear the slot at a given index in O(1) time.
requirements.rationale: Clearing a slot via the pointer Reset operation ensures no stale data remains, preventing downstream consumers from reading invalid values.

requirements.title: Array Bounds Checking
requirements.description: The array shall return JUNO_STATUS_OOB_ERROR when an index is outside its capacity.
requirements.rationale: Explicit bounds checking prevents out-of-bounds memory access, which would cause silent corruption or undefined behavior on bare-metal targets lacking an MMU.

requirements.title: Array Vtable Validation
requirements.description: The array module shall verify that the array API vtable entries are non-null.
requirements.rationale: Validates the vtable at runtime to ensure memory safety before dispatching any operation through function pointers.

requirements.title: Array Capacity Validation
requirements.description: The array module shall verify that the array capacity is non-zero.
requirements.rationale: A zero-capacity array has no backing storage and cannot perform any useful operation; rejecting it early prevents division-by-zero or empty-loop errors in dependent modules.
